This is the first paragraph of a long test document.
It contains multiple sentences that will be used to test the chunker.
The chunker should split this document into several chunks.
Each chunk should stay within the configured token limit.

This is the second paragraph of the long test document.
It provides additional content to ensure we can generate multiple chunks.
The overlap between chunks should be visible in consecutive chunks.
Overlap tokens help maintain context across chunk boundaries.

This is the third paragraph of the long test document.
It introduces new topics to challenge the chunking algorithm.
Semantic coherence is important for retrieval-augmented generation.
Chunks that preserve sentence boundaries are more readable.

This is the fourth paragraph of the long test document.
It continues to build on the previous topics with more detail.
The chunking algorithm must handle paragraphs of varying lengths.
Some paragraphs may be long while others are quite short.

This is the fifth and final paragraph of the long test document.
It wraps up all the content that has been presented so far.
After chunking, each chunk should have sequential indices.
The metadata from the original document should be preserved.
